# SESSION BRIEF · BU-admin — Admin scaffolding

*Brief version: 1.0 · Author: Paul · Date: April 2026*

---

## Objective

Build the generic admin surface — a schema-driven UI that lets admins
and queue managers do real work (moderate, resolve, edit, approve) on
every existing entity. One implementation, all entities. Success looks
like: Sharon logs in and can see a list of every entity type, tap into
one, view rows, edit rows, resolve work items, grant/revoke roles. The
interface is plain but operational. **Members never see any of this.**

**This session is likely too big for one run.** The brief explicitly
marks suggested checkpoints. Following `session-hygiene.md`, commit at
each checkpoint and hand off cleanly if context gets tight.

---

## Scope

### Build in this session

**Server layer:**
- `/server/lib/trpc.ts` (modify — add `requireRole` middleware if not present)
- `/server/lib/auth.ts` (new — minimal auth stub; see "Known gotchas")
- `/server/routers/admin/index.ts` (new — admin router aggregator)
- `/server/routers/admin/entities.ts` (new — generic entity CRUD procedures)
- `/server/routers/admin/work-items.ts` (new — queue-specific procedures)
- `/server/routers/admin/role-grants.ts` (new — role grant/revoke)
- `/server/routers/admin/coordinator-profile.ts` (new — coordinator identity admin)
- `/server/routers/_app.ts` (modify — register the admin router)
- `/server/services/admin.ts` (new — shared admin service logic, entity-metadata-driven queries)
- `/server/services/audit.ts` (new — write-only audit log service; used by admin procedures)

**Client layer:**
- `/app/admin/layout.tsx` (new — admin-section wrapper, auth gate)
- `/app/admin/page.tsx` (new — entity list with row counts)
- `/app/admin/[entity]/page.tsx` (new — generic entity list view)
- `/app/admin/[entity]/[id]/page.tsx` (new — generic entity detail view)
- `/app/admin/[entity]/[id]/edit/page.tsx` (new — generic entity edit form)
- `/app/admin/[entity]/new/page.tsx` (new — generic entity create form)
- `/app/queue/page.tsx` (new — work-items queue UI, two-tab split)
- `/app/queue/[id]/page.tsx` (new — work-item detail with claim actions)
- `/app/admin/roles/page.tsx` (new — role grant management)
- `/app/admin/coordinators/page.tsx` (new — coordinator profile management)

**Shared components (under `/components/admin/`):**
- `/components/admin/EntityList.tsx` (new — schema-driven table)
- `/components/admin/EntityDetail.tsx` (new — schema-driven field display)
- `/components/admin/EntityForm.tsx` (new — schema-driven form)
- `/components/admin/RelationField.tsx` (new — FK-dropdown component)
- `/components/admin/EnumField.tsx` (new — enum-dropdown component)
- `/components/admin/JsonField.tsx` (new — JSONB display + edit)
- `/components/admin/ClaimAvatar.tsx` (new — avatar + name + claim age)
- `/components/admin/RoleBadge.tsx` (new — visual role indicator)

**Tests:**
- `/tests/integration/admin-entities.test.ts` (new — CRUD integration tests for two sample entities)
- `/tests/integration/work-items.test.ts` (new — claim/lease/resolve flow tests)
- `/tests/integration/role-grants.test.ts` (new — grant/revoke flow tests)
- `/tests/unit/admin-service.test.ts` (new — entity-metadata-driven query logic tests)

**Documentation:**
- `/docs/architecture/admin-surface.README.md` (new — implementation-level notes on how the scaffolding actually works)
- `/server/routers/admin/README.md` (new — structure of the admin routers)

### Do NOT touch

- `/prisma/schema.prisma` — schema is locked; any changes need an ADR
- `/eslint-rules/**` — F06 rules apply, don't modify
- `/eslint.config.js` — extend only if strictly necessary (e.g., adding admin/ to a glob); surface as open question first
- Any existing feature code outside admin/queue paths
- `/docs/product/` or `/docs/process/` — decisions are made
- `/docs/architecture/admin-surface.md` — this session IMPLEMENTS it, doesn't modify it
- `/package.json` — don't add major new dependencies without surfacing first (small utilities OK)

### Out of scope for this session

- **Real authentication.** The auth stub (`server/lib/auth.ts`) is a placeholder that reads a user ID from a cookie / header for dev. Real NextAuth / Auth.js / Lucia integration is a separate Build Unit.
- **Member-facing UI.** No feed, no composer, no post pages. Those are separate Build Units (BU-composer, BU-feed, etc.).
- **WebSocket / real-time updates.** The queue page polls on interval; true real-time is Phase 2.
- **Push notifications.** Queue managers get no alerts; they check the queue. Phase 2.
- **Mobile admin.** Desktop-first; mobile is Phase 2.
- **Per-type work-item resolution forms.** The generic form works for all types in MVP. Rich per-type forms (vetting-specific UI, flag-specific UI) come with each work-item type's Build Unit.
- **Admin analytics dashboards.** Counts on the admin index page are sufficient; deeper analytics use PostHog (per D037).
- **Presence indicators** ("who's viewing this"). Parking-lot per `admin-surface.md`; claim avatar is in-scope, presence is not.
- **Bulk action UX polish.** Basic checkboxes + action menu is enough; advanced bulk patterns are Phase 2.

## Contracts

### Inputs consumed

- `/docs/architecture/admin-surface.md` — the full spec; this session IMPLEMENTS it
- `/docs/architecture/claim-and-lease.md` — WorkItem lifecycle, atomic claim, sweeper spec
- `/docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — especially D040, D042, D036, D038
- `/prisma/schema.prisma` — the entities this session exposes
- `/server/admin/entity-metadata.ts` — the metadata map drives the generic UI
- `/server/admin/entity-metadata.README.md` — entry shape and extension pattern
- `/docs/process/api-contract-discipline.md` — the 10 rules every procedure follows
- `/docs/product/copy-library.md` — honest copy for all UI text
- `/docs/product/design-philosophy.md` — the 5 principles; applies to admin UI too

### Outputs produced

These become contracts future sessions consume.

- **`requireRole(role)` middleware** — exports from `server/lib/trpc.ts`. Future procedures use this for auth-gating.
- **`auditLog(action, entityType, entityId, details)` service** — exports from `server/services/audit.ts`. Every Build Unit that mutates data calls this.
- **Generic admin components** — under `/components/admin/`. Future entities automatically get UI by adding an entry to `entity-metadata.ts`.
- **`/admin` and `/queue` routes** — the UX conventions established here propagate to future admin features.
- **Role middleware pattern** — `.use(requireRole(...))` chains become the standard; F06's `no-inline-auth-check` rule enforces this.
- **Audit log shape** — what fields get written for what kinds of events.

---

## Acceptance criteria

**Functional criteria (each must be verifiable by manual click-through):**

- [ ] `/admin` lists all entity types with row counts for each
- [ ] `/admin/user` shows a table of users with columns per `entityMetadata.user.listColumns`
- [ ] `/admin/user/[id]` shows the user's full detail
- [ ] `/admin/user/[id]/edit` lets an admin edit the user; changes are saved
- [ ] Same pattern works for `region`, `coordinatorProfile`, `group`, `roleGrant`
- [ ] `/queue` shows two tabs: "Available" (unclaimed) and "In Progress" (claimed)
- [ ] Clicking a work-item opens `/queue/[id]` with claim action visible
- [ ] Clicking Claim atomically assigns the item; other queue managers see it claimed within one refresh
- [ ] While claimed, heartbeat keeps the lease alive (verify: claim an item, wait 60s, refresh → lease extended)
- [ ] Self-release returns the item to Available
- [ ] Admin force-release works with required reason
- [ ] Resolve marks the item complete; it disappears from both tabs
- [ ] `/admin/roles` shows active role grants; admin can grant a new role with reason; admin can revoke with reason
- [ ] Revoking a role auto-releases any work items the user had claimed
- [ ] `/admin/coordinators` lists coordinator profiles with their groups
- [ ] Auth middleware rejects unauthenticated requests with 401
- [ ] Queue manager role rejects admin-only actions (feature flags, role grants) with 403
- [ ] Every mutation writes an audit log entry (verify: check AuditLog table after a series of admin actions)

**Non-functional criteria:**

- [ ] All procedures follow api-contract-discipline rules (no `z.any()`, middleware-based auth)
- [ ] F06 lint rules pass on all new code (headers, no PII, no inline auth checks, no z.any)
- [ ] TypeScript strict passes; no `any`; no `@ts-ignore`
- [ ] Every file has `@build-unit BU-admin` header (per D038)
- [ ] `npm run test` passes including new integration + unit tests
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes with zero violations
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes with zero errors
- [ ] `npx prettier --check .` passes
- [ ] Admin surface visually honours design tokens (bordeaux for destructive actions, neutral for standard)
- [ ] Copy is honest (per design-philosophy.md and copy-library.md — no "Click here to manage!" marketing voice)
- [ ] Error states are visible (not silent): failed grants show the error, failed claims show who got there first, etc.
- [ ] Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, form labels are correct, focus management is sensible
- [ ] Admin section is visually marked as "admin" so queue managers and admins know they're in a privileged context

---

## Permission matrix

| Action | Member | Queue manager | Admin |
|---|---|---|---|
| Access `/admin/*` | ✗ (401 / redirect) | ✓ | ✓ |
| View entity lists | ✗ | ✓ (read-only default) | ✓ |
| Edit entities | ✗ | ✗ (unless entityMetadata permits) | ✓ |
| Create entities | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| Delete entities | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| Access `/queue` | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Claim work item | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Release own claim | ✗ | ✓ (own) | ✓ |
| Force-release others' claims | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| Resolve work item | ✗ | ✓ (own claimed) | ✓ |
| Grant role | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| Revoke role | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| View audit log | ✗ | ✓ (entries involving them) | ✓ (all) |
| Manage coordinator profiles | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |

Enforcement: `requireRole` middleware per procedure. Never inline checks
(enforced by F06 rule 4). Row scoping at the query layer where needed.

---

## UI states

Key states each major page must handle:

### `/admin` (entity index)
- **Default:** list of entities with counts
- **Loading:** skeleton rows
- **Empty entity:** "No records yet" per entity
- **Permission denied:** redirect to home with flash message

### `/admin/[entity]` (entity list)
- **Default:** table with rows
- **Loading:** skeleton rows
- **Empty:** "No records yet — [create new] if applicable"
- **Search active:** filtered rows; "No matches" if empty
- **Error loading:** error banner with retry
- **Deleted filter:** toggle to show soft-deleted rows

### `/admin/[entity]/[id]` (detail)
- **Default:** all fields read-only, edit button visible (if admin)
- **Loading:** skeleton
- **Not found:** 404 with "this entity may be soft-deleted — view deleted?"
- **Permission denied:** redirect with flash

### `/queue`
- **Available tab:** unclaimed items, sorted by priority+age
- **In Progress tab:** claimed items with claimant avatar
- **Loading:** skeleton
- **Empty Available:** "Nothing to do — good work" (honest but warm)
- **Empty In Progress:** "Nobody claiming anything right now"

### `/queue/[id]` (detail)
- **Unclaimed:** Claim button prominent
- **Claimed by me:** Release / Resolve buttons
- **Claimed by someone else:** View only; "Claimed by Sharon, 12m ago"
- **In Review:** active heartbeat within last 5 min — subtle indicator
- **Resolved:** read-only view showing resolution + resolver
- **Abandoned:** read-only, banner explaining why

### `/admin/roles`
- **Default:** tabular view of active grants
- **Grant form:** user-search + role-picker + reason field
- **Confirmation:** "Grant queue_manager to Sharon Cohen? Reason: [...]"
- **Revoke confirmation:** "Revoke admin from Paul? Reason required. Claimed items will auto-release."
- **Special states:** cannot revoke own admin; cannot revoke last admin (button disabled with explanation)

---

## Tests required

**Integration tests:**
- Entity CRUD for 2 sample entities (`user`, `region`): list, get, create, update, soft-delete, restore
- Work-item lifecycle: create → claim → heartbeat → self-release; claim → resolve; claim → force-release; claim → TTL expiry
- Role grant lifecycle: grant → use → revoke → verify access removed; verify claimed items auto-released on revoke

**Unit tests:**
- `admin.service.ts`: entity-metadata-driven list, get, filter functions
- `audit.service.ts`: the `auditLog()` function writes correct entries; extracts change diffs correctly
- Atomic claim: simulate race condition (two claims within 1ms of each other) — only one wins

**Not required:**
- E2E browser tests (manual click-through is sufficient for MVP)
- Performance benchmarks
- Accessibility auditing (verify manually with keyboard)

---

## Scenarios to verify against

Read `/docs/product/scenarios.md` and verify these work end-to-end through
the admin surface:
- **SCN-04 (Sharon flags a post)** — verify: the flag appears in the queue; a queue manager can claim it; resolving it writes audit entries
- **SCN-08 (Vetting flow)** — verify: a vetting work item can be claimed and resolved; the applicant's `verifiedAt` gets set on approval
- **SCN-Coordinator admin** — verify: admin can view Sharon's coordinator profile, add a group, edit reach estimate
- **SCN-Role grant** — verify: admin can grant Sharon queue_manager role; Sharon's session updates; she can now access `/queue`

Each scenario should click-through successfully in a dev environment
before the session is declared done.

---

## Known gotchas

### Auth stub

The brief explicitly **does not** build real auth. Instead, create a
minimal dev stub at `/server/lib/auth.ts`:

```typescript
// server/lib/auth.ts
/**
 * @build-unit BU-admin
 * @spec architecture/admin-surface.md
 *
 * DEV-ONLY auth stub. Real auth lands in a separate Build Unit.
 * Reads `x-dev-user-id` header in dev, looks up the user.
 *
 * DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION.
 */
export async function getCurrentUser(req: Request): Promise<User | null> {
  if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') {
    throw new Error('Auth stub cannot run in production. Real auth required.');
  }
  const userId = req.headers.get('x-dev-user-id');
  if (!userId) return null;
  return prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id: userId } });
}
```

Then `requireRole` middleware uses this to resolve the user.

This lets you build and test the admin surface without blocking on auth
decisions. Real auth is a separate session.

### `requireRole` middleware

Create in `/server/lib/trpc.ts`:

```typescript
export const requireRole = (role: SystemRole | 'admin' | 'queue_manager') => 
  t.middleware(async ({ ctx, next }) => {
    if (!ctx.user) throw new TRPCError({ code: 'UNAUTHORIZED' });
    const hasRole = await userHasActiveRole(ctx.user.id, role);
    if (!hasRole) throw new TRPCError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN' });
    return next({ ctx: { ...ctx, user: ctx.user } });
  });
```

All admin procedures chain `.use(requireRole('admin'))` or
`.use(requireRole('queue_manager'))`. Never inline.

### Entity-metadata-driven everything

The generic components read `entityMetadata` at the top of each page
and render accordingly. **Do not write per-entity UI code.** If an
entity needs specific rendering (e.g., a custom field type), extend
the generic components to handle it, don't fork them.

### The sweeper / cron job

The WorkItem TTL + 4-hour-cap sweeper (per claim-and-lease.md) runs
every 5 minutes. For MVP, either:
- A Vercel cron job (recommended; set up via `vercel.json`)
- A manual admin action to run the sweep (less ideal but simpler)

If you can't decide, surface as an open question.

### Zod schemas as source of truth

Per api-contract-discipline rule 6, each tRPC procedure's input Zod
schema is the source of truth. The `EntityForm` component reads these
schemas to generate form fields. This means: when you define a mutation,
the matching form field is auto-generated. No duplication.

### Honest copy

Admin copy should follow design-philosophy principle 5:
- ❌ "Successfully granted admin role!"
- ✅ "Sharon is now admin."

- ❌ "Oops! Something went wrong."
- ✅ "Grant failed: Sharon already has admin role."

- ❌ "Are you sure you want to delete this?"
- ✅ "Soft-delete this Region? It will be hidden but can be restored."

### The `server/admin/` directory

F06 added `server/admin/**/*.ts` to the header-rule glob. All new files
under `server/admin/` get headers. But note: the admin routers live
under `server/routers/admin/`, NOT `server/admin/`. `server/admin/`
contains only the entity-metadata map. Be careful not to confuse these.

---

## Suggested checkpoints

This session is large. Per `session-hygiene.md`, commit at these
natural boundaries:

**Checkpoint 1 — Auth stub + middleware (30-45 min)**
- `/server/lib/auth.ts`, `/server/lib/trpc.ts` updated, test that
  middleware rejects unauthenticated requests
- Commit: `feat(admin): auth stub + requireRole middleware`

**Checkpoint 2 — Audit service (30 min)**
- `/server/services/audit.ts` + tests
- Commit: `feat(admin): audit log service`

**Checkpoint 3 — Generic entity CRUD (1-2 hours)**
- `/server/routers/admin/entities.ts`, `/server/services/admin.ts`
- `/app/admin/*`, `/components/admin/EntityList.tsx`, etc.
- Integration tests for User + Region CRUD
- Commit: `feat(admin): generic entity scaffolding (list, detail, edit, create)`

**Checkpoint 4 — Work items queue (1 hour)**
- `/server/routers/admin/work-items.ts`
- `/app/queue/*`, `/components/admin/ClaimAvatar.tsx`
- Integration tests for claim/release/resolve
- Commit: `feat(admin): work items queue with claim-and-lease`

**Checkpoint 5 — Role grants (45 min)**
- `/server/routers/admin/role-grants.ts`
- `/app/admin/roles/*`
- Integration tests for grant/revoke
- Commit: `feat(admin): role grant management`

**Checkpoint 6 — Coordinator profiles + final polish (30 min)**
- `/server/routers/admin/coordinator-profile.ts`
- `/app/admin/coordinators/*`
- Final validation pipeline
- Commit: `feat(admin): coordinator profile admin + final BU-admin polish`

**If context runs tight before Checkpoint 6:**
Stop, write handoff doc per `session-hygiene.md`, commit what's done,
push the branch, open a PR even if incomplete. The next session
continues from the handoff.

- [ ] All files in "Build" list present; none in "Don't touch" modified
- [ ] All 6 checkpoints committed (or handoff doc if incomplete)
- [ ] All functional acceptance criteria verified by click-through
- [ ] All non-functional criteria met
- [ ] Every file has `@build-unit BU-admin` header
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint clean
- [ ] Typecheck clean
- [ ] Prettier clean
- [ ] No `TODO` or `// FIXME` in committed code (except the auth stub's "DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION" warning, which is intentional)
- [ ] Open questions list populated with judgement calls
- [ ] PR opened against main with summary of what shipped

---

## Open questions to surface

Pre-identified:

1. **Sweeper implementation.** Vercel cron (`vercel.json` entry), application-level interval, or manual admin-triggered sweep? Recommend Vercel cron. Surface for confirmation.

2. **Soft-delete restore UI.** The brief requires restore as an action but doesn't prescribe exact UI. Propose a pattern.

3. **Bulk actions.** The entityMetadata map has `bulkActions` but the brief is vague on UX. Propose: basic checkbox + action dropdown with confirmation dialog. Confirm scope.

4. **Role-grant session invalidation.** When a role is revoked, the user's session should stop working within 60 seconds. Depends on session implementation (auth stub has none; real auth will). Note as a dependency on the real auth Build Unit.

5. **Queue page polling interval.** The heartbeat interval is 60s; the list-refresh interval could be 5-10s. Confirm or propose.

6. **Search UI in EntityList.** Full-text search per `entityMetadata.searchableFields` — basic input with 300ms debounce, or something richer? Recommend basic.

7. **Existing lint config extension for admin paths.** If `/app/admin/`, `/components/admin/`, or `/server/routers/admin/` aren't yet in F06's globs, they need to be. Surface the glob extension as a config change.

8. **Auth stub's cookie vs header.** The stub reads `x-dev-user-id` from headers. Should it also support a dev cookie so the browser persists identity between page loads? Recommend yes for usability.

9. **Error boundary pattern.** Where should React error boundaries go? Per-route, per-section, or one at the admin layout? F11 in Phase 0 covers error boundaries generically; is this session's responsibility to scaffold them or does F11 do that?

---

## What this brief does NOT cover

1. **Real authentication** (NextAuth / Auth.js / Clerk / Lucia) — separate Build Unit
2. **Member-facing UI** (feed, composer, post detail) — BU-composer, BU-feed
3. **WebSocket / real-time updates** — Phase 2
4. **Per-work-item-type resolution forms** — one per type; each its own Build Unit
5. **Admin analytics beyond row counts** — PostHog
6. **Bulk action UX polish** — basic works; richer is Phase 2
7. **Mobile admin** — Phase 2
8. **Per-entity custom rendering** — generic should suffice; if an entity genuinely needs custom UI, it's a Build Unit extension

---

## Slice convention

BU-admin is the **first feature-building Build Unit**. Convention established
here propagates:

- New entities (added via ERD slices) automatically get admin UI by
  updating `entity-metadata.ts`
- New routers register via `_app.ts` with the same patterns
- Generic components are extended (not forked) for new needs
- Every mutation writes audit log entries
- Every procedure uses middleware for auth (F06 enforces)

Future Build Units (BU-vetting Vetting, BU-composer Composer, etc.) inherit
this convention. They don't build their own admin; they add entity-metadata
entries and the generic scaffolding does the work.

---

## What lands in MVP after this session

- Sharon can log in (via the dev auth stub with her user ID)
- Sharon sees `/admin` and `/queue`
- She can claim a work item, resolve it, see the audit trail
- Jeremy can grant Sharon queue_manager role
- Paul can grant Jeremy admin role
- All the Slice 1 and Slice 1.5 entities are admin-accessible
- The scaffolding is ready for BU-vetting+ to layer features on top
